crooked_lawn.speaker: Karen
crooked_lawn.text: "The lawn looks... creative." She says 'creative' like it's a felony. "Did you mow it drunk?"

choices.text: "The mower has a mind of its own"

crooked_excuse.text: "Organic. Mowing." She closes her eyes. Doug stifles a laugh from across the yard. "At least you MOWED it," she concedes. Progress.

crooked_blame.text: "That mower is five years old. It works fine when I use it." She has never used the mower. But the point stands.

normal.speaker: Karen
normal.text: "Hey babe. How was your day?" She searches your face for clues. She always searches your face for clues.

karen_ok.text: She nods. For once, she seems satisfied. This might be the best moment of your entire marriage.

karen_suspicious.text: "'Saturday stuff.'" She repeats it slowly. Making a mental note. Karen always makes mental notes. She has volumes.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: She stops mid-sentence. Her finger rises. It points. It accuses.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: "What. Color. Is. That. On. Your. Collar."

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "It's — ha — it's ketchup! BBQ sauce! A rogue cherry tomato!"

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: She does not speak. She stares. A sparrow outside falls silent.
The temperature in the backyard drops four degrees. Guests check their phones and leave.

lipstick_lie.text: "Ketchup. Doesn't. Come. In. That. Color." She walks away. Your mother-in-law is already on the phone.

lipstick_excuse.text: "Which neighbor?" You hesitate one second too long. Karen files this away in the permanent record.

perfume.speaker: Karen
perfume.text: She hugs you, then freezes. Sniffs. "Why do you smell like vanilla and regret?"

perfume_dodge.text: "Doug doesn't use air freshener. His car smells like beef jerky and broken dreams." She knows Doug too well.

perfume_deny.text: "You don't—" She closes her eyes. Counts to ten. Only gets to four. Walks away.

smell.speaker: Karen
smell.text: She gets close. Sniffs. "You smell... weird. Like incense? Since when do you burn INCENSE?"

smell_excuse.text: "A cleansing ritual." She stares at you. "In the suburbs." You shrug. She walks away to get more wine.

smell_charcoal.text: "Kingsford doesn't smell like patchouli, David." Fair point.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: She scans you top to bottom. Like a TSA agent. With grievances.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: You sway. Imperceptibly. Very perceptibly. The lawn tilts.

beats.speaker: Karen
beats.text: "Three. Two. One. In front of my MOTHER, David."

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "I'm — I'm so — the grill needed me."

wasted_response.text: "We're going to talk about this. AFTER everyone leaves. Go stand by the grill and try not to fall in it."

wasted_act.text: You straighten your polo. Stand tall. Almost topple over. Karen catches you by the elbow. "Grill. Now. Don't talk to anyone."

buzzed.speaker: Karen
buzzed.text: "Have you been drinking?" She asks it like a lawyer who already knows the answer.

choices.text: "Just one with Doug. It's a BBQ!"

buzzed_ok.text: She sighs. "Just... pace yourself. My mom is HERE." She hands you a water bottle with the intensity of a hostage negotiator.

buzzed_bad.text: "I will DEFINE it for you later. With a PowerPoint. In the bedroom. Where you'll be sleeping. On the floor."

lawn_angry.speaker: Karen
lawn_angry.text: "The lawn." Two words. The temperature drops ten degrees. Guests stop mid-conversation.

lawn_excuse.text: "You had TEN HOURS." She's doing the math. She always does the math. The math is not in your favor.

lawn_tomorrow.text: "Tomorrow." She says it like she's tasting poison. "Tomorrow." Your mother-in-law shakes her head from across the yard.
